case_studies.name: Pwani Oil Digital Transformation
case_studies.result: 340% increase in qualified leads within 90 days
case_studies.brief: Complete digital transformation for Kenya's leading cooking oil manufacturer, including e-commerce platform, SEO strategy, and analytics infrastructure.
case_studies.challenge: Legacy website was not mobile-responsive, had zero organic search visibility, and no e-commerce capability. Manual order processing was creating bottlenecks.
case_studies.solution: Built a Next.js e-commerce platform with Stripe payments, implemented comprehensive SEO strategy targeting commercial keywords, and integrated analytics for data-driven decisions.

case_studies.name: Mombasa Continental Resort Visibility
case_studies.result: 180% increase in direct bookings, reduced OTA dependency
case_studies.brief: Digital strategy for luxury beach resort to increase direct bookings and reduce reliance on Online Travel Agencies.
case_studies.challenge: Over 70% of bookings coming through expensive OTAs. Poor local SEO meant missing 'hotels in Mombasa' searches. Website did not reflect luxury positioning.
case_studies.solution: Redesigned brand identity and website with luxury aesthetic, implemented local SEO strategy, created content marketing funnel, and built direct booking incentives.

case_studies.name: Coast General Hospital Digital Presence
case_studies.result: 250% increase in appointment bookings via website
case_studies.brief: Patient-centered digital platform for leading Mombasa hospital with online appointment system and health resources.
case_studies.challenge: Patients struggling to find department information. Phone lines overwhelmed with appointment requests. No online presence for specialized services.
case_studies.solution: Built accessible website with online appointment booking, department directories, doctor profiles, and health resources. Implemented WCAG accessibility standards.

research_articles.name: The Golden Ratio in Modern Web Design
research_articles.excerpt: How mathematical principles like the golden ratio (1.618) create naturally pleasing visual hierarchies that guide user attention.

content.text: The golden ratio appears everywhere in nature—from nautilus shells to spiral galaxies. In web design, this same 1.618 proportion creates layouts that feel inherently balanced and guide users through content naturally.

The golden ratio, represented by the Greek letter phi (φ), is approximately 1.618. When a line is divided into two parts such that the whole length divided by the long part is equal to the long part divided by the short part, you have the golden ratio.

Modern applications include using golden rectangles for hero sections, fibonacci sequences for spacing systems, and spiral layouts for storytelling. The result is interfaces that feel organic and intuitive.

research_articles.name: Technical SEO for East African Markets
research_articles.excerpt: Site speed, Core Web Vitals, and technical infrastructure considerations for businesses targeting Kenya, Tanzania, and Uganda.

content.text: While content is king, technical SEO is the foundation. For East African businesses, specific considerations around hosting, CDN selection, and mobile optimization are critical for search visibility.

In Kenya and Tanzania, over 85% of web traffic comes from mobile devices. Technical SEO strategies must prioritize mobile performance above all else. This means compressed images, minimal JavaScript, and fast server response times.

research_articles.name: 2024 Digital Marketing Trends for African Businesses
research_articles.excerpt: WhatsApp Business API, mobile money integration, and AI-powered personalization are reshaping how African businesses connect with customers.

content.text: The African digital landscape is evolving rapidly. Businesses that adapt to new channels and payment methods are capturing market share from slower competitors.

WhatsApp Business API is becoming the primary customer service and sales channel for African SMEs. Integration with e-commerce platforms allows customers to browse, ask questions, and pay—all within WhatsApp.
